title: Federal Law No. 11: CONCERNING PROCUREMENT IN THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT
text:
Federal
Law No. 11

Issued
on 27/11/2023

Corresponding
to 13/05/1445 H.

CONCERNING
PROCUREMENT IN THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT



We,
Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the United Arab Emirates State,

-
Pursuant to the perusal of the Constitution;

-
Federal Law No. (1) of 1972 on Competencies of Ministries and Powers of
Ministers, and its

amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (5) of 1985 on the issuance of the Civil Transactions Law, and
its

amendments;

-
Federal Law No. (8) of 2011 concerning the Re-organisation of the Supreme Audit
Institution ;


-
Federal Law No. (2) of 2014 on the Small and Medium Enterprises;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (9) of 2016 on Bankruptcy, and its amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (13) of 2016 on the establishment of the Federal Tax
Authority, and its

amendments;


-
Federal Decree-Law No. (7) of 2017 on the Excise Tax, and its amendments,

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (8) of 2017 on the Value Added Tax (VAT), and its
amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (11) of 2017 on granting certain powers to the Council of
Ministers;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (9) of 2018 on Public Debt, and its amendments; 

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (15) of 2018 on the Collection of Revenues and Public
Assets; 

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (16) of 2018 on Federal Government Real Estate
Property;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (26) of 2019 on the Public Finance, and its
amendments;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (31) of 2021 on promulgating the Crimes and Penalties
Law;

-
Decree-Law No. (32) of 2021 on Commercial Companies;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (38) of 2021 on Copyrights and Neighbouring Rights;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (28) of 2022 on Tax Procedures;

-
Federal Decree-Law No. (47) of 2022 on Corporate and Business Taxation;
and

-
Based on the proposal of the Minister of Finance, the approval of the Council of
Ministers and

the
Federal National Council and the ratification of the Federal Supreme
Council,

Have
issued the following Law:

Chapter
1

Definitions
and Objectives of the Law

Article
1 - Definitions

In
application of the provisions of this Law, the following terms and expressions
shall have the meanings assigned to them, unless the context requires
otherwise:

State:
The United Arab Emirates.

Government:
The Federal Government of the State.

Ministry:
The Ministry of Finance

Minister:
The Minister of Finance.

Delegation
of Authority (DoA) Matrix: Document, prepared by the Ministry, that specifies
the powers of employees authorised to approve financial and non-financial
decisions related to government purchases and the financial limits associated
with them, in accordance with the procurement methods set out in this Law and
the Regulations implementing it. This document shall be issued by a decision of
the Minister.

Federal
Entities: Ministries established by virtue of Federal Law No. (1) of 1972 on
Competencies of Ministries and Powers of Ministers and its amendments, and the
Federal Government agencies and authorities classified in Article (3) of this
Law.

Purchases:
Any product, service, or business needed by Federal Entities.

Procurement:
The set of procedures undertaken by the Federal Entity to secure its Purchases
in accordance with the provisions of this Law and Regulations implementing
it.

Annual
Procurement Plan: The plan prepared by the Federal Entity to determine its
requirements and needs for Procurement in a way that achieves the public
interest for the subsequent fiscal year.

Emergency
Purchases: Purchasing in emergency situations to obtain necessary Purchases,
when following purchasing procedures and methods in normal cases leads to an
unacceptable delay to the relevant Federal Entity in securing such
Purchases.

Procurement
System: The electronic or digital system or any system used in the Federal
Entity that regulates Procurements, allows suppliers and Federal Entities to
communicate, interact, and conclude contracts securely, and enables Federal
Entities to announce Purchase orders and receive quotations from suppliers in
accordance with the provisions prescribed in this Law and the Regulations
implementing it.

Public
Interest: Any direct or indirect benefits that accrue to the Federal Entity, the
Government, and the society as a result of the Procurement, and contribute to
achieving the Government’s strategic visions and priorities.

Public
Value: The best result that the Federal Entity can achieve in exchange for money
by optimally exploiting resources following an effective and economic
method.

Supplier:
Any natural or legal person who provides Purchases in accordance with the
provisions of this Law and the Regulations implementing it.

Participating
Supplier: Any natural or juristic person submitting an offer on a new bid or bid
opportunity related to a request to provide Purchases to a Federal Entity.

Confidential
Information: Confidential information specified by this Law and the guidelines
implementing it.

Sensitive
Commercial Information: Information that may affect the Supplier’s
commercial interests if disclosed, including profit margins and new innovations,
as determined by this Law and the guidelines implementing it.

Tender
Announcement: Statement published on the Procurement System announcing a new
Procurement opportunity in accordance with the provisions specified by this Law
and the Regulations implementing it.

Public
Tender: An invitation or request for Purchases that a Federal Entity announces
to the general public through the Procurement System, which is intended to
provide equal opportunities to all Suppliers interested in submitting their
offers to supply the required Purchases.

Implementing
Regulations: The regulations and policies issued by the Council of Ministers or
its delegate to implement this Law.

Guidelines:
Guidelines and procedures, for Purchases, issued by the Minister to implement
the provisions of this Law and the Regulations implementing it.

Article
2 - Objectives of the Law 

1-
This Law shall regulate the general framework for Purchases in the
Government.

2-
This Law shall aim to achieve the following:

a-
Determining the principles, controls and standards for government
Procurements.

b-
Promoting and directing Procurements in the Federal Entities in line with the
Government's digital agenda.

c-
Activating digital Procurement Systems and implementing the best world-leading
policies and practices at all stages of Procurement.

d-
Ensuring equality, fair treatment and non-discrimination among all Participating
Suppliers.

e-
Involving all relevant stakeholders and participants in the Procurement at
Federal Entities and ensuring that they implement operations efficiently and
effectively to ensure quality and achieve spending efficiency.

f-
Promoting integrity, trust, transparency, and competition in all Procurement
procedures while providing public value and public interest that takes into
account quality criteria, standards, sustainable development, and supporting
small and medium enterprises and the national economy.

g-
Providing flexibility, improving the performance of procurement and contracting
operations, and enhancing the efficiency of planning and Procurement management
at the federal level.

Chapter
2

Scope
of Application and Exceptions

Article
3- Scope of Application of the Law

The
provisions of this Law shall apply to all Procurement and contracting activities
and procedures at the following Federal Entities:

1-
Ministries and Central Agencies: Ministries established pursuant to Federal Law
No. (1) of 1972 and its amendments concerning Competencies of Ministries and
Powers of Ministers, and the councils, agencies and entities of equivalent
status that exercise federal executive, legislative and judicial powers.

2-
Independent Federal Entities: Federal Entities already established or currently
being established in accordance with the legislation in force in the State with
the aim of carrying out specific Government tasks or services and operating
under the supervision of the Government, which: -

a-
Are granted, by the law or decision establishing it, independent legal
personality and the capacity to act, prepare, organise and implement its own
budget.

b-
Have their own administrative and organisational structure.

c-
Are non-profit organisations.

Article
4 - Exceptions from the Scope of Enforcement of the Law

1-
The provisions of this Law shall not apply to:

a-
The Ministry of Defence and the Armed Forces.

b-
State security entities.

c-
Federal Entities engaged under international agreements or obligations or with
international organisations related to the Purchases carried out by these
Entities.

d-
Construction projects and contracts.

e-
Purchases of a military or security nature carried out by the Ministry of
Interior or any Federal Entity of a security or military nature, and approved by
the Minister or the head of the relevant Federal Entity.

f-
Purchases for the State’s representative missions abroad.

g-
Purchases made outside the State for the purpose of using them abroad.

h-
Purchases related to establishing, furnishing and equipping Government offices
outside the State.

i-
Purchases related to medicines and medical supplies.

j-
Partnership contracts between Federal Entities and the private sector that are
classified as “partnership projects” and are subject to the
provisions governing partnership projects in accordance with the relevant
regulating legislation.

k-
Purchases and leases of existing lands and buildings, and operations of selling,
destroying, and disposing of assets and inventory that are subject to relevant
legislation, provisions, and the relevant regulatory policies.

l-
Investment operations carried out by authorised Federal Entities in accordance
with the relevant regulating provisions and legislation, which aim to achieve a
commercial return or financial profit for the Entity and are not for the purpose
of securing its needs and business, and include the purchase and sale of stocks,
bonds, currencies, and the like.

m-
Financing operations and the provision of guarantees by authorised Federal
Agencies in accordance with the relevant regulating provisions and
legislation.

n-
Federal Entities and purchases that are excluded according to the decision of
the Council of Ministers after taking the Ministry’s opinion.

2-
The exceptions contained in this article shall not exempt the relevant Federal
Entity from being guided by the provisions of this Law, ensuring to take into
account its objectives, and striving to achieve the best public value for its
purchases.

Chapter
3

Governance
and Competencies

Article
5 - Governance of the Procurement Process

This
Law and the Regulations implementing it shall regulate the provisions for the
governance of the Procurement, including the role and responsibilities of
Federal Entities, councils, committees, and organisational units concerned with
them.

Article
6 - DoA Matrix

1-
Decisions concerning the Procurement shall be issued in accordance with the DoA
Matrix.

2-
Each Federal Entity shall determine, by a decision of its head or persons of
similar status, the employees vested with the powers contained in the DoA Matrix
issued by the Ministry.

3-
Federal Entities may review their (DoA) Matrix and request that the Ministry
amend it according to their needs without prejudice to the general framework of
the DoA Matrix issued by the Ministry.

4-
Independent Federal Entities may adopt their own DoA Matrix, guided by the DoA
Matrix issued by the Ministry.

Article
7- Obligations of Federal Entities

Federal
Entities shall be committed to the following:

1-
Working on advance planning and determining its procurement needs, including
preparing the annual Procurement plan and seeking to achieve the public
interest.

2-
Developing and implementing mechanisms and plans to support the provision of
procurement at the highest levels of quality and ensuring the provision of the
best public value.

3-
Choosing the appropriate Procurement method in light of the needs, risks,
complexities, and supply strategy related to the targeted Purchases.

4-
Working to reduce the costs incurred when implementing the annual Procurement
plan, taking into account achieving the best overall value.

5-
Managing comprehensive Procurements for Purchases, starting from defining
requirements to awarding procedures and signing contracts, with the involvement
of the necessary organisational units.

6-
Ensuring compliance and harmonisation of Procurement processes with the
principle of transparency at all stages, including full transparency in
disclosing bid evaluation criteria.

7-
Working to attract the best Suppliers, ensuring fairness and non-discrimination
in treatment among Participating Suppliers, and encouraging competition.

8-
Creating opportunities to attract and encourage small and medium enterprises,
national procurement, local suppliers, and supporting the localisation policy,
green companies, sustainable commercial activities, or products that reduce
carbon emissions and environmental damage.

9-
Acting as a Supervisory Body for all contracts, requests, purchase orders, and
amendment requests, and other relevant purchasing documents.

10-
Managing post-contractual activities such as disputes and amendments, following
up on Supplier performance, assessing risks, and appointing the appropriate
staff to manage them.

11-
Any other obligations determined under the regulations implementing this
Law.

Chapter
4

Procurement
System and Purchasing Principles

Article
8- Procurement System

1-
Purchases shall be tendered through the Procurement System unless the Law or the
Regulations implementing it stipulate otherwise. Independent Federal Entities
may tender their purchases through the electronic or digital Procurement System
or any system used in the Federal Entity.

2-
The regulations implementing this Law shall specify the procedures for using the
Procurement System, including correspondence and communication on the electronic
system, signing contracts, and other relevant matters that have the necessary
legal effect.

3-
The Procurement System shall have the highest levels of privacy,
confidentiality, security, information transparency and ease of use, while
ensuring the integrity of procedures.

4-
The Procurement System shall allow Suppliers to view information and data
related to bids specified by the Law and the Regulations implementing it.

Article
9- Transparency and Competition

1-
All Procurement procedures shall be subject to the principles of transparency,
freedom of competition and equal opportunities at all stages and shall be
implemented in accordance with the rules and procedures stipulated in this Law
and the regulations implementing it.

2-
In order to achieve the principle of transparency and equality of opportunities,
the relevant Federal Entity shall be committed to informing the Participating
Suppliers of all major information and decisions related to the tender or
affecting fair competition among them.

Article
10- Integrity

Each
Federal Entity shall adopt mechanisms that enhance the integrity of its
Procurement activities and procedures, including at a minimum the
following:

1-
Ensuring that all Procurement procedures are sound and take into account the
public interest.

2-
Ensuring that all employees concerned with Procurements are committed to
integrity, impartiality, and the provisions of the Law.

3-
Ensuring that the participation of any Federal Entity employee in the
procurement operations of the Federal Entity to which they belong, whether
directly or indirectly, or through companies or institutions they own, or in
which they are partners or agents, is prohibited.

4-
Ensuring awareness of the employees in charge of the Procurement, as to how to
detect and report cases of conflict of interest or their potential occurrence
and deal with them, in accordance with what is prescribed by the regulations
governing this regard.

Article
11- Equality

The
Federal Entity shall take into consideration, in the Procurement procedures, not
to discriminate among Participating Suppliers, unless it decides to restrict
participation to specific categories in accordance with what is stipulated by
the Law and the Regulations implementing it.

Article
12- Protection of Suppliers’ Information

1-
Federal Entities shall undertake to protect Suppliers’ confidential
information and sensitive commercial information, including information
affecting fair competition among Participating Suppliers.

2-
The Federal Entity may not disclose confidential information or sensitive
commercial information except in the following cases: -

a-
With the written approval of the Participating Supplier concerned.

b-
In the event of necessary disclosure in accordance with the provisions of the
Law, an international agreement, a judicial order, or an order from a regulatory
authority.

c-
In the event of a need for limited disclosure, which shall be announced in the
Tender Announcement documents and legally approved by the Participating
Suppliers through their participation in the tender.

d-
Any other cases or provisions stipulated in the regulations implementing this
Law.

Article
13 - Public Interest

1-
Federal Entities shall take into account the extent to which Public Interest can
be achieved when offering any of their Purchases.

2-
Public interest includes benefits that help achieve the Government’s
strategic visions and priorities, such as creating commercial opportunities for
local companies and commercial activities, supporting small and medium
enterprises, national products, local suppliers, green companies, sustainable
commercial activities, or products that reduce carbon emissions and
environmental damage. The Regulations implementing this Law shall explain the
special provisions and preferential features concerning the public interest in
Procurements.

Article
14 - General Value of Purchases

1-
Purchasing decisions shall be based on achieving the highest overall value and
securing the best possible outcomes against the cost of Procurement throughout
the Procurement cycle and the duration of the contract and in accordance with
the provisions of this Law and the Regulations implementing it.

2-
The general value of purchases will be achieved if:

a-
The costs correspond to the total benefits of the Purchases.

b-
They contribute to achieving the results that the Federal Entity seeks to
achieve in terms of purpose, quality, etc.

c-
They achieve the public interest of the State, whether social, environmental,
economic, cultural, or otherwise.

Article
15- Procurement Planning

1-
Each Federal Entity shall prepare an annual Procurement plan in conjunction with
the process of annual budget preparation. The plan shall include developing a
strategy to secure the needs of the Federal Entity based on a study and analysis
of market conditions, the proposed method for supplying the necessary purchases,
and the risks associated with it. The plan shall include an estimated budget for
costs, expected timetables, evaluation standards and criteria and public
interest that the Federal Entity seeks to achieve through Procurement. The
Federal Entity may amend its Procurement plan during the fiscal year in
accordance with its priorities and requirements.

2-
Federal Entities shall, before offering their purchases, assess the public
interest to be achieved and whether the purchases contribute to achieving any of
the Government’s priority strategic outputs.

3-
The Federal Entity may publish information about its Procurement plan for the
coming months or years, including key information about its work and Purchases
for that period. The Federal Entity may also publish a prior announcement about
any potential Purchases.

4-
Publishing any information about the relevant Federal Entity’s Procurement
plan shall not entail any obligation requiring it to offer those
Purchases.

Chapter
5

Purchase
Provisions and Methods

Article
16 - Purchase Methods

1-
Federal Entities shall put their Purchases in a public tender announced through
the Procurement System, and they may use other Procurement methods in accordance
with the provisions specified by the Law and the Regulations implementing it,
provided that this does not lead to limiting competition.

2-
Taking into account the provisions of Clause (1) of this Article, Federal
Entities shall choose the best purchasing method that achieves the highest
overall value for their Purchases.

3-
The Regulations implementing this Law shall specify all types of purchasing
methods and their provisions and procedures.

4-
When the Federal Entity wishes to solicit offers, it may pre-qualify the
Participating Suppliers to ensure that the necessary qualifications and
capabilities are met, including their technical capabilities, financial and
administrative capabilities, the scale of their obligations, and their ability
to perform.

Article
17- Announcement of Tender and Purchases

1-
The Federal Entity shall launch Procurement bids publicly unless the conditions
for other bidding methods specified by the Regulations implementing this Law are
met.

2-
The following steps at a minimum shall be followed for the advertisement:

a-
Publishing the announcement of the Procurement or tender opportunity via the
electronic or digital Procurement System or any other system used by the Federal
Entity.

b-
Providing the Participating Suppliers with access to all relevant bid
documents.

Article
18- Content of the Tender Announcement

The
Tender Announcement shall contain all the information that Participating
Suppliers need to prepare and submit their offers, including the
following:

1-
The name of the relevant Federal Entity.

2-
Clarifying the purchasing method used.

3-
Providing a description of the required purchases, their technical
specifications, plans and drawings, if any, in addition to instructions and
outputs.

4-
Determining the required or estimated quantities of Purchases.

5-
The terms and conditions of contracting and contract duration.

6-
The levels of service and implementation that the Supplier is required to
achieve.

7-
The evaluation mechanism and criteria and the importance of each
criterion.

8-
The deadline for bid submission and bidding procedures on the Procurement
System.

9-
Any other data that the Federal Entity deems necessary to include in the
Announcement.

Article
19- Subscription Period and Response to the Advertisement

1-
Participating Suppliers shall be given sufficient time to respond to the Tender
Announcement, and the Federal Entity shall determine that time realistically,
taking into account all relevant factors, including the nature and complexity of
Procurement and the volume of information and details required to prepare the
offers.

2-
The Subscription Period shall not be less than the periods specified in the
Regulations implementing this Law in all cases, and the Federal Entity may
extend the period if it deems it necessary.

3-
If the Federal Entity issues clarifications or amendments to the Tender
Announcement, the deadline for submitting responses may be extended in a way
that allows Participating Suppliers to take them into consideration.

Article
20 - Participating Suppliers’ Inquiries and Requests and Responses to
Them

1-
Participating Suppliers may raise inquiries and request reasonable and necessary
data to evaluate the bid within the period specified in the Tender
Announcement.

2-
The Federal Entity shall respond to all reasonable inquiries and requests as a
matter of urgency. If it is unable to respond within a reasonable time that
enables Participating Suppliers to submit their responses, it may decide to
extend the time-limit for submitting responses.

3-
All bidding Participating Suppliers shall be informed of the inquiries received
by the Federal Entity regarding the offer and its answers in this regard.
However, bidders may not be informed of the source of the inquiry or request or
the confidential information of one of the Participating Suppliers and sensitive
commercial information.

4-
A meeting may be held with the Participating Suppliers before submitting the
final responses, upon the request of any of them, to provide general information
about the project and clarify inquiries (if any). Notes for these meetings shall
be recorded and made available to all Participating Suppliers.

Article
21- Cancellation or Modification of the Tender Announcement

1-
The Federal Entity may cancel or amend the procedures for offering or announcing
the Tender at any time before the time-limit for submitting responses, in
accordance with the conditions and procedures specified by the Regulations
implementing this Law, and it shall not be permissible to open any of the bids
submitted after the cancellation decision.

2-
The Federal Entity shall publish on the Procurement system and inform all
Suppliers participating in the Tender at the same time of any cancellation or
change that occurs to the requirements of the Tender or the Tender
Announcement.

3-
The Federal Entity shall provide sufficient time for Participating Suppliers who
have submitted offers to respond to the amendments, and if it deems it
necessary, to extend the deadline for submitting offers to enable them to amend
their responses.

Article
22- Bid Evaluation Criteria

1-
Offers regarding bids shall be evaluated in a transparent and fair manner using
evaluation criteria related to the subject-matter of Procurement, which may
include the following:

a-
Financial cost.

b-
The cost of operating and maintaining Purchases.

c-
Duration of delivery and completion of Purchases.

d-
Compliance of Purchases with the required characteristics and specifications
(technical, environmental, practical, or otherwise).

2-
Provisions for payments and guarantees.

f-
The experience, reputation, competence and professionalism of the Supplier
participating in providing the type of Purchases required.

g-
Any other evaluation criteria stipulated in the Regulations implementing this
Law.

2-
A margin of preference may be granted for Participating Suppliers who achieve
the best public interest or added national value, or in favour of locally
produced Purchases, or to stimulate local small and medium enterprises, in
accordance with what is determined by the Regulations implementing this
Law.

3-
Non-price evaluation criteria shall be objective and commensurate with the
nature of the Purchases to be offered, and be quantitatively measurable to the
extent possible.

4-
The Tender Announcement shall state clearly and in detail the evaluation
criteria and mechanism used and the importance that each criterion
represents.

5-
Bids shall be evaluated based on the evaluation standards, and the evaluation
mechanism and criterion previously announced in the Tender Announcement. It
shall not be permissible to use any standards or procedures that have not been
announced in accordance with the provisions of this article.

Chapter
6

Awarding
Provisions and Controls

Article
23 - Negotiation

1-
The Federal Entity may negotiate with Participating Suppliers to reach a
technical or Financial Agreement in the cases specified by the Regulations
implementing this Law.

2-
Negotiating with Participating Suppliers shall be a mandatory step in the supply
process if the total value of the contract is higher than the value prescribed
by the Regulations implementing this Law.

Article
24 - Disqualification of Suppliers and Cancellation of the Award Decision

1-
The Federal Entity may disqualify Participating Suppliers from the tender for
any of the following reasons:

a-
If the bid submitted by the Participating Supplier does not meet the minimum
basic requirements specified in the Tender Announcement.

b-
The failure of the Supplier participating in the implementation of preliminary
Procurements proposed by one of the Federal Entities or the fulfilment of any of
the cases of ceasing to deal with the Supplier specified by the Regulations
implementing this Law.

c-
If the Participating Supplier fails to fulfil his financial obligations towards
the Government, including taxes, fees, etc.

d-
If the Participating Supplier does not adhere to the timetables stated in the
request for proposals.

e-
Any other reason specified by the Regulations implementing this Law.

2-
The Federal Entity shall have the right to cancel the award decision if the
winning Supplier commits a serious error that affects transparency, integrity,
or competition, such as achieving any of the following: -

a-
If the Supplier engages in unethical practices or provides incorrect data.

b-
It is proven that the bid violates any of the legislation in force in the
State.

c-
If the award results in a negative impact on national security or the
confidentiality of sensitive government information.

d-
Offering bribery, illegal commissions, bid rigging, fraud, or abuse of power
with the aim of illegally influencing the procedures of the Procurement, without
prejudice to the provisions of any other relevant law or legislation.

3-
The Federal Entity shall inform the disqualified Participating Supplier of the
disqualification decision and the reasons on which its decision was based.

4-
The Regulations implementing this Law shall determine the authority having
competence to issue the disqualification decision in the Federal Entity.

Article
25 - Disqualification of an Offer due to Low Price

1-
It shall not be permissible to disqualify any offer due to a low price unless
the Federal Entity is convinced that the offer price is significantly low
compared to the estimated cost and prevailing market prices, which may affect
the ability of the Participating Supplier to fulfil his contractual obligations
towards the Federal Entity, provided that the relevant Federal Entity, after
reviewing the estimated prices, discusses with the Participating Supplier with
the low offer, and asks him to provide details of the components that make up
his offer and explain the reasons for his low offer. If it is not convinced of
his ability to fulfil its contractual obligations, it may reject the
offer.

2-
The Federal Entity shall inform the disqualified Supplier of the
disqualification decision and the reasons on which its decision was based.

Article
26 - The Awarding

The
bid shall be awarded in accordance with the procedures specified by the
Regulations implementing this Law and based on the evaluation criteria contained
in the Tender Announcement. The bid shall be awarded to the Participating
Supplier with the bid that meets the following conditions: -

1-
Fulfilling the requirements and conditions of the contract and proving the
ability and capabilities necessary to implement the contract in accordance with
the required provisions.

2-
Achieving the public interest and the best public value throughout the contract
period.

Article
27- Informing Suppliers of the Decision Awarding the Bid

1-
After evaluating the offers and making the decision awarding the bid, all
Participating Suppliers shall be informed of the decision awarding the bid to
the winning Supplier.

2-
If the winning Supplier is late in submitting the required documents or signing
the contract without an acceptable excuse within the period specified by the
Regulations implementing this Law, the Federal Entity may, after warning him,
cancel the award, and award the bid to the next best Participating Supplier or
decide to re-offer it.

Article
28 - Publication of the Award Decision

Details
of the award decision may be published on the Procurement System within (30)
thirty days from the completion of the awarding and signing of the contract. The
publication shall include all the main data of the bid in accordance with what
is specified in the Regulations implementing this Law.

Article
29- Justifications of the Award Decision

Without
prejudice to the right of the Participating Supplier to file a grievance before
the relevant Federal Entity within the time limits stipulated in Article (38) of
this Law, every Participating Supplier who is not a winner in the awarding may
request the Federal Entity to clarify the reasons that led to him not being
selected, including strengths and weaknesses in his offer. If the Participating
Supplier requests clarification, he shall be responded to within the period
specified in the Regulations implementing this Law.

Chapter
7

Contracting
Provisions and Controls

Article
30 - Procurement Contract

The
Procurement Contract shall regulate the provisions of the Contract and the
obligations and rights of its parties. It shall be prepared in a manner that
does not contravene this Law and the Regulations implementing it. In the event
of any disputes or disagreements between the Federal Entity and the Supplier,
reference shall be made to the Procurement Contract as it is the legal document
regulating the contractual relationship between them.

Article
31- Preparation of Procurement Contracts

1-
The Regulations implementing this Law shall specify the types and forms of
standard Procurement Contracts.

2-
The Federal Entities may formulate contracts for their purchases if necessary,
according to the nature and complexity of the Purchases.

Article
32 - Signing the Contract

The
Procurement Contract shall be signed between the Federal Entity and the winning
Supplier after being informed of the award decision and submitting a performance
bond if required, in accordance with the procedures and forms specified by the
Regulations implementing this law.

Article
33 - Amendment of the Contract

1-
When there is a need to amend the contract or make any changes to the offer
(such as amending the project scope, schedule, cost, or contract terms and
conditions), the provisions, procedures, and maximum limits stipulated in the
Regulations implementing this Law shall be taken into account and the required
approvals shall be obtained in accordance with the DoA Matrix.

2-
Any request to amend a Procurement Contract shall be based on acceptable reasons
and justifications, and the availability of the necessary financial
appropriations for any amendment shall be verified, regardless of its
value.

Article
34 - Assignment of the Contract and Subcontracting

1-
The contracting Supplier may not assign the contract or any part of it to
another supplier or subcontract the work except after obtaining prior written
approval from the Federal Entity. The Regulations implementing this Law shall
specify the relevant conditions and controls in this regard.

2-
The Federal Entity may provide payments directly to the sub-supplier, and the
Regulations implementing this Law shall determine the relevant conditions and
controls in this regard.

3-
The contracting Supplier - in all cases - shall be responsible, jointly with the
sub-supplier, for implementing the contract.

Article
35 - Payment to the Suppliers

1-
Federal Entities shall be committed to settling payments to Suppliers in
accordance with the provisions and dates stipulated therein.

2-
In the event of subcontracting, the contracting Supplier shall undertake to
settle payments to the sub-suppliers as soon as they are due as agreed upon,
provided that the payment terms for the sub-suppliers are compatible with the
payment terms agreed upon between the main Supplier and the relevant Federal
Entity.

Article
36 - Intellectual Property

If
the Procurement includes the establishment of new intellectual property rights
that are expected to benefit Federal Entities, the Tender Announcement shall
clearly state the purpose of these rights and whether the Federal Entity intends
to own them, obtain a licence to use them, or exploit them for its activities in
the future.

Article
37- Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution

1-
The laws in force in the State shall be applicable to the Procurement Contract.
It shall be permissible, based on the approval of the Minister or head of the
relevant Federal Entity, as the case may be, to agree to apply a foreign law to
contracts that are executed outside the State.

2-
The parties shall implement their obligations in the contract in accordance with
its terms and conditions without prejudice to this Law and the Regulations
implementing it. If any party fails to implement the obligations thereof, the
other party may resort to the competent courts in the State.

3-
The Regulations implementing this Law shall determine other means of settling
disputes resulting from the implementation of a Procurement contract concluded
in accordance with the provisions of this Law and the procedures and conditions
of resorting to them.

Chapter
8

Considering
Complaints

Article
38 - Grievance Against Decisions of the Federal Entity

1-
Every Participating Supplier shall have the right to file a grievance before the
Federal Entity regarding any decision it took before the issuance of the award
decision, within (5) five working days from the date of notifying him of the
decision. He shall also have the right to file a grievance before the Federal
Entity regarding the award decision, within (5) five working days from the date
of issuance of the award decision.

2-
The Federal Entity shall decide on the grievance in an impartial manner within
the period specified in the Regulations implementing this Law. If that period
passes without responding to the grievance, this shall be considered a rejection
of the grievance.

3-
The Regulations implementing this Law shall specify the mechanisms and controls
for filing the grievance, responding to it, the relevant time limits, the
grievance procedures, and the authority having competence to decide on it.

Chapter
9

General
Provisions

Article
39 - Language of Procurement Contracts and Tender Documents

1-
Contracts, tender documents, their annexes, and related communications shall be
drafted in Arabic. English may be used instead if the Federal Authority deems it
necessary.

2-
The Procurement Contract shall include a clarification of the language adopted
for interpretation and implementation, and determine the specifications, plans,
and correspondence related to it when two languages are used in drafting the
contract or document.

Article
40- Contracting Between Federal Entities

Federal
Entities and wholly government-owned companies, whether at the local or federal
level, may contract with each other by direct agreement without being subject to
this Law either substantively or procedurally. However, they shall themselves
undertake to implement the works or secure Purchases, and they may also act on
behalf of each other in initiating contracting procedures.

Article
41 - Exception from the Provisions of the Law

The
Council of Ministers may make an exception to any provision of this Law if the
need arises for that and upon the request of the relevant Federal Entity,
provided that the subject-matter and justification of the exception are
specified.

Article
42- Regulations Implementing this Law

1-
The Council of Ministers shall issue the Regulations implementing this Law,
including the following:

a-
The detailed terms, procedures and conditions of the Procurement on the
Procurement System.

b-
The roles of the organisational units concerned with Procurement in Federal
Entities.

c-
Specifying the tasks and roles of Procurement Committees in Federal
Entities.

d-
Determining the methods, categories and levels of Procurement and the provisions
related to them.

e-
Provisions related to the Procurement DoA Matrix.

f-
Specifying purchase operations and detailed provisions related to them.

g-
Determining the types and forms of contracts.

h-
Cases of non-conformity.

i-
Forms used in Procurements.

j-
Any provisions, procedures or guidelines intended to regulate Procurement
operations in Federal Entities and determine the entities concerned with issuing
them.

2-
The Minister shall issue the guidelines and procedures necessary to implement
the provisions of this Law and its implementing Regulation.

3-
Independent Federal Entities may adopt their own Procurement regulations or
policies in accordance with the provisions of this Law, provided that their
preparation is guided by the Regulations implementing this Law.

Article
43 - Abrogation

1-
Any provision that contradicts or conflicts with the provisions of this Law
shall be abrogated.

2-
Cabinet Decision No. (1/1f) of 2022 concerning the adoption of the digital
procurement policy for the Federal Government, and Cabinet Decision No. 4 of
2019 on the Procurement Regulation and Storehouse Management, shall remain
applicable to the extent that they do not conflict with the provisions of this
Law, until the regulations and guidelines implementing this Law and any other
relevant policies are issued.

Article
44- Contracts Concluded Prior to the Date of this Law

Purchases
of Federal Entities concluded before the effective date of this Law shall be
excepted from the application of its provisions. They shall be subject to the
provisions of their contracts and the legislation applicable to them before the
issuance of this Law. They may not be amended, renewed, or extended except in
accordance with the provisions of this Law.

Article
45 - Publication and Enforcement of the Law

This
Law shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force as of
the day following its publication.

Issued
by us at the Presidential 
Palace in Abu Dhabi 
On: 13/05/1445
H.
Corresponding to: 27/11/2023 


Mohammed bin
Zayed Al Nahyan

President of the
United Arab Emirates

Gazette, Issue No. 764 (Annex), p. 27.



[1] 
This Federal Law was abrogated by virtue of Clause (3) of Article (42) of
Federal Decree-Law No. 56 dated 2/10/2023 on the UAE Accountability Authority.
The same Clause stipulated that the laws, regulations and decisions issued
there-under shall remain applicable until the issuance of laws, regulations and
decisions in implementation of the provisions of this Decree-Law, to the extent
that they are not contrary to its provisions; thus, a mention is in order.
